cards.back: A situation where two or more processes are blocked, each waiting for the other to release a resource
A situation where a process is unable to get access to a resource, even though it needs it and other processes are holding onto it for a longer time
A situation where two or more processes are unable to proceed because they are constantly updating the same resource, never actually making any progress
A situation where a lower-priority process can outwait a higher-priority process, often due to the operating system's resource allocation policies
A situation where a process is unable to proceed because it is waiting for a resource, but is not actually doing any work, often due to a lack of CPU resources
